steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The subject is “Puerto Rico's museums” (noun phrase), and the predicate is “showcase everything from the territory's art to its architecture to its coffee” (verb phrase). Together they form a single independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb punctuation rule
steps.content: No punctuation should separate a subject from its verb. The correct answer is “museums” with no punctuation, allowing the subject to connect directly to the verb “showcase.” All other choices violate this rule by inserting punctuation between the subject and verb. That matches choice A — A is correct.
